summaryText: Guest Speaker Montell Jordan opened his message, 'The Name of God,' by emphasizing the profound importance of names in accessing identity, inheritance, and relationship. He illustrated this with a personal story of meeting actor Laurence Fishburne, highlighting the difference between knowing someone from a distance and being known by them personally. Pastor Montel then applied this principle to our relationship with God, explaining that while we often use the generic term 'God,' He has many personal names that reveal different aspects of His character and provide specific access to His power. The core of the message centered on Exodus 3:14, where God reveals His name as 'I AM'—a declaration of His eternal, ever-present nature. Pastor Montel taught that our proximity to God, or closeness in relationship, is revealed by what we call Him, moving from a distant 'Father God' to intimate names like Jehovah Jireh (Provider) or Jehovah Rapha (Healer). He stressed that through Jesus Christ, who is 'the way, the truth, and the life,' the gap between humanity and God is closed, allowing for a personal, powerful relationship. The sermon concluded with an invitation to receive salvation and to draw near to God, calling on His specific names for our specific needs, because He knows us by our name and calls us holy, righteous, and His children.
mainPoints: Names are extremely important; they determine access, identity, and the depth of a relationship, which applies to knowing God personally versus knowing about Him.
God has many names (e.g., Yahweh, Jehovah Jireh) that describe His character and provide specific access to His power; our proximity to Him is revealed by what we call Him.
Through Jesus Christ, the 'I AM,' we have direct access to God the Father, moving from a distant, religious relationship to a close, personal one where we are known and called by name.

versesMentioned.text: Then Moses replied to God, 'I'd go to the people of Israel and say to them, "The God of your ancestors has sent me to you." They will ask me, "What is his name?" What should I tell them?' God answered Moses, 'I am who I am. This is what you must say to the people of Israel: "I am has sent me to you."' Again God said to Moses, 'This is what you must say to the people of Israel: "The Lord God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, Isaac, & Jacob, has sent me to you. This is my name forever. This is my title throughout every generation."'
In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
'I am the Alpha and the Omega,' says the Lord God, 'who is, and who was, and who is to come, the Almighty.'
Jesus answered, 'I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.'

Monday.scripture: Exodus 3:14 (NIV) - 'God said to Moses, "I AM WHO I AM. This is what you are to say to the Israelites: 'I AM has sent me to you.'"'
Monday.action: Begin your day by acknowledging God's presence with you right now. Say, 'God, you are my I AM for this moment.'
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you that you are not a distant God of the past or future, but you are with me now. Help me to live in the reality of your present presence today. Amen.

Tuesday.scripture: Psalm 9:10 (NIV) - 'Those who know your name trust in you, for you, Lord, have never forsaken those who seek you.'
Tuesday.action: Choose one specific name of God (e.g., Jehovah Shalom - The Lord is Peace). Meditate on what that name means for a current situation in your life.
Tuesday.prayer: Jehovah Shalom, you are my peace. I bring my anxiety about [mention a situation] to you right now. I trust in your name and receive your peace. Amen.

Wednesday.scripture: John 14:6 (NIV) - 'Jesus answered, "I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me."'
Wednesday.action: In a moment of need or decision today, consciously approach God in prayer by saying, 'Father, I come to you in the name of Jesus.'
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, thank you for being the way. I confess I need you to bridge the gap to the Father. I come to you now, asking for your guidance and grace. Amen.

Thursday.scripture: Philippians 2:9-10 (NIV) - 'Therefore God exalted him to the highest place and gave him the name that is above every name, that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow...'
Thursday.action: Identify an area where you feel powerless. Pray a simple, direct prayer for help, ending with '...in the mighty name of Jesus.'
Thursday.prayer: Father, I feel overwhelmed by [name the challenge]. I ask for your strength and intervention. I claim your power and authority over this situation in the mighty name of Jesus. Amen.

Friday.scripture: Isaiah 43:1 (NIV) - 'But now, this is what the Lord says—he who created you, Jacob, he who formed you, Israel: "Do not fear, for I have redeemed you; I have summoned you by name; you are mine."'
Friday.action: Reflect on the truth that God calls you His child, not just His creation. Write down or declare: 'Today, I am known by God as [His son/His daughter].'
Friday.prayer: Heavenly Father, thank you for knowing me intimately and calling me by name. Help me to live today in the security of being your beloved child, not just a distant creation. Amen.

Saturday.scripture: Psalm 145:18 (NIV) - 'The Lord is near to all who call on him, to all who call on him in truth.'
Saturday.action: Set aside time for personal, conversational prayer. Instead of formal language, talk to God as a close Father or Friend about your week.
Saturday.prayer: Dad, it's me. Thank you for walking with me this week. Here's what's on my heart... [share openly]. I draw near to you, trusting you are close to me. Amen.
